_comment: Source of truth for live nodes. The report-live-quotes workflow reads this, runs `uq check` against each non-null endpoint, and writes docs/status/nodes.json. Fill in `endpoint` (https://host[:port]/) once a VM is provisioned and serving stage 1.

nodes.label: AWS Nitro Enclave
nodes.chain: nitro attestation doc (COSE_Sign1) -> aws nitro root (pinned)
nodes.note: PCR0 from `uq check` matches `nitro-cli build-enclave` PCR0 exactly

nodes.label: Azure Confidential VM (SEV-SNP, vTPM)
nodes.chain: vTPM HCL -> SNP report -> VCEK -> ASK -> ARK-Milan (pinned)
nodes.detail: azure cvms run amd sev-snp under the vTOM paravisor and do not expose /dev/sev-guest. the paravisor publishes a genuine snp report through the vTPM (NV index 0x01400001); `uq azure` extracts it and verifies it against the AMD root (per-chip VCEK -> ASK -> ARK-Milan). report_data == sha256(runtime-data) endorses the vTPM AK, which then signs a tpm2 quote binding value_x. value_x is the sha256 of attestation-service, built inside this same cvm by a self-hosted github runner and carrying github build provenance (sigstore) for the same digest. two roots (sigstore + amd) meet at one value_x. trust chains to amd silicon, not to MAA.
nodes.source_provenance: github: maceip/attestation-service .github/workflows/attest-in-tee.yml (self-hosted, in-TEE) -> release azure-tee-build; verify with scripts/verify-source-to-silicon.sh
nodes.transport: attested-tls: the leaf cert carries the snp->amd bundle at the tcg-dice cmw oid (2.23.133.5.4.9); the cert spki is bound into the ak quote (qualifyingData = sha256(spki||value_x)), so the channel itself is attested with no ca in the chain.

nodes.label: GCP Confidential VM (Intel TDX)
nodes.detail: gcp c3 confidential vms expose intel tdx via configfs-tsm, which the uq tdx collector already supports. provisioning is gated on gcloud auth + a gcp project not present in this environment; deploy/gcp-tdx.sh is ready to run.
